From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

International men's basketball club tournament in Europe

For the women's league, see [EuroCup Women](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Women "EuroCup Women").

Basketball league

BKT EuroCup[![Image 4](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/c3/Eurocup_new_logo.png/250px-Eurocup_new_logo.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Eurocup_new_logo.png)
Organising body[Euroleague Basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball")
Founded 7 July 2002; 23 years ago(2002-07-07)[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_note-1)
First season**ULEB Cup**

[2002–03](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2002%E2%80%9303_ULEB_Cup "2002–03 ULEB Cup")

**Eurocup**

[2008–09](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Eurocup_Basketball "2008–09 Eurocup Basketball")

**EuroCup**

[2016–17](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_EuroCup_Basketball "2016–17 EuroCup Basketball")
Region[Europe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Europe "Europe")
Number of teams 20
Level on [pyramid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/European_professional_club_basketball_system "European professional club basketball system")2
Related competitions[EuroLeague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroLeague "EuroLeague") (1st tier)
Current champions[![Image 5: Israel](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/Flag_of_Israel.svg/40px-Flag_of_Israel.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel "Israel")[Hapoel Tel Aviv](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hapoel_Tel_Aviv_B.C. "Hapoel Tel Aviv B.C.") (1st title) 

([2024–25](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024%E2%80%9325_EuroCup_Basketball "2024–25 EuroCup Basketball"))
Most championships[![Image 6: Spain](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/9/9a/Flag_of_Spain.svg/40px-Flag_of_Spain.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spain "Spain")[Valencia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valencia_Basket "Valencia Basket") (4 titles)
TV partners[tv.euroleague.net](https://tv.euroleague.net/)
Website[euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup)
![Image 7](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/48/Basketball_current_event.svg/40px-Basketball_current_event.svg.png)_[2025–26 EuroCup Basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%9326\_EuroCup\_Basketball "2025–26 EuroCup Basketball")_

**EuroCup Basketball**, commonly known as the **EuroCup** and currently called **BKT EuroCup** for sponsorship reasons, is an annual professional [basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") club competition organized by [Euroleague Basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball").

Founded as **ULEB Cup** in 2002, the competition lasted until 2008 when a new competition was introduced after an agreement between [ULEB](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ULEB "ULEB") and [FIBA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA "FIBA") under the name of _EuroCup_ for the [2008–09 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Eurocup_Basketball "2008–09 Eurocup Basketball"), following a change in format.[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_note-2) Given that the [FIBA EuroChallenge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA_EuroChallenge "FIBA EuroChallenge") was known as EuroCup until 2008, a new era of stronger cooperation between ULEB and FIBA Europe was set in 2008. The number of teams in the new competition was increased to a total of 48 and the winner of the 4th tier [FIBA EuroCup Challenge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA_EuroCup_Challenge "FIBA EuroCup Challenge"), formerly known as EuroCup would get an automatic qualification for the tournament's following season, for first time.

Though initially advertised as a new competition, the ULEB Cup and EuroCup Basketball are now considered the same competition, with the change of name being simply a re-branding.

Since the 2021–22 season both EuroCup finalists qualify for next season's EuroLeague. Until then only the winner was entitled to the one year licence.

The title has been won by 16 clubs, 3 of which have won the title more than once. The [most successful club](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#Performance_by_club) in the competition are [Valencia Basket](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valencia_Basket "Valencia Basket"), with four titles. The current champions are [Hapoel Tel Aviv](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hapoel_Tel_Aviv_B.C. "Hapoel Tel Aviv B.C."), winning their first title after defeating [CB Gran Canaria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CB_Gran_Canaria "CB Gran Canaria") in the 2025 Finals.

## History

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The competition was created in 2002, as the ULEB Cup. In 2008 after an agreement with [FIBA Europe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA_Europe "FIBA Europe") the name changed to EuroCup, while the then 3d tier competition, FIBA EuroCup became known as [EuroChallenge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroChallenge "EuroChallenge"). The two finalists of the latter would earn promotion to EuroCup (2008-15).

It has had several names:

*   [2002–03](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2002%E2%80%9303_ULEB_Cup "2002–03 ULEB Cup") to [2007–08](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_ULEB_Cup "2007–08 ULEB Cup")**ULEB Cup**
*   [2008–09](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Eurocup_Basketball "2008–09 Eurocup Basketball") to [2015–16](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Eurocup_Basketball "2015–16 Eurocup Basketball")**Eurocup**
*   [2016–17](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_EuroCup_Basketball "2016–17 EuroCup Basketball") to present **EuroCup Basketball**

### Sponsorship names

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Sponsorship names")]

On 7 July 2016, [Chipita](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chipita "Chipita") and Euroleague Basketball announced a strategic agreement to sponsor the European competition across the globe. According to the agreement, starting with the 2016–17 season, the competition would be named 7DAYS EuroCup. This title partnership was set to run for three seasons.[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_note-3)

### Logos

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Logos")]

| **Evolution of the EuroCup logo** |
| --- |
| **2002–2008** | **2008–2016** | **2016–2023** | **2023–present** |
| [![Image 8](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/1/10/Cup_uleb.png/120px-Cup_uleb.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Cup_uleb.png) | [![Image 9](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/4e/ULEB_Eurocup_logo.png/250px-ULEB_Eurocup_logo.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:ULEB_Eurocup_logo.png) | [![Image 10](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/3/31/EuroCup_Basketball_logo.svg/250px-EuroCup_Basketball_logo.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:EuroCup_Basketball_logo.svg) | [![Image 11](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/c3/Eurocup_new_logo.png/120px-Eurocup_new_logo.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Eurocup_new_logo.png) |

## Qualification

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Qualification")]

Clubs qualify for the competition based on their performance in their domestic leagues competitions. For this purpose, the clubs from countries participating in the [ABA League](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ABA_League "ABA League") qualify for the competition based on their performance in the ABA League, and not their domestic leagues.

## Format

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Format")]

Starting with the [2016–17 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_EuroCup_Basketball "2016–17 EuroCup Basketball"), the EuroCup's first phase is the **Regular Season**, in which 20 teams participate. The participants include 20 clubs automatically entered into the Regular Season. Each team plays two games (home-and-away) against every other team in its group. At the end of the Regular Season, the field is cut from 20 to 16. The next phase, known as the **Top 16**, then begins, featuring the 16 survivors of the Regular Season in four-team groups. As in the Regular Season, each Top 16 group is contest in a double [round-robin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Round-robin_tournament "Round-robin tournament") format. The group winners and runners-up advance to the third phase, the **Playoffs**. Each playoff series is [best-of-three](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Playoff_format#Best-of-three_playoff "Playoff format"), and the winners of each series advance to the next round persistently until the Finals. Home advantage in the series goes to the best placed team in the Top 16. The **Finals** features the two remaining series winners in a [best-of-three](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Playoff_format#Best-of-three_playoff "Playoff format") series with home advantage in the series to the best placed team in the Top 16.

### Previous EuroCup formats

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Previous EuroCup formats")]

Historically, the competition began with a group phase in which the starting field was reduced to 16 teams. The survivors then advanced to a knockout phase. In the inaugural [2002–03 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2002%E2%80%9303_ULEB_Cup "2002–03 ULEB Cup"), the knockout phase consisted entirely of two-legged ties. In the following [2003–04 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2003%E2%80%9304_ULEB_Cup "2003–04 ULEB Cup"), the final became a one-off game, but all other knockout ties remained two-legged.

In the [2007–08 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_ULEB_Cup "2007–08 ULEB Cup"), the initial phase, now called the Regular Season, was only used to reduce the field to 32 teams. The survivors were paired into two-legged knockout ties, with the winners advancing to another set of two-legged ties. The survivors then entered the first-ever Final Eight phase in the competition's history, consisting of one-off knockout games.

The following [2008–09 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Eurocup_Basketball "2008–09 Eurocup Basketball"), was the first in which preliminary rounds were conducted. That year saw two preliminary rounds held, the first involving 16 teams, and the second involving the eight winners, plus eight teams that had received byes into that round. The survivors of the second preliminary round joined 24 direct qualifiers in the Regular Season. This season also saw the introduction of the Last 16 group phase, and proved to be the last for the Final Eight.

The last stage of the EuroCup, the EuroCup Finals, was reduced from eight teams to four, starting with the [2009–10 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2009%E2%80%9310_Eurocup_Basketball "2009–10 Eurocup Basketball"). This stage was directly analogous to the [EuroLeague Final Four](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroLeague_Final_Four "EuroLeague Final Four"), and like that stage of the [EuroLeague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroLeague "EuroLeague"), consisted of one-off knockout semifinals, followed by a single-game final. Unlike the EuroLeague Final Four, in which the third-place game and final are held two days after the semifinals, the corresponding games of the EuroCup were held the day after the semifinals.

In the [2012–13 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Eurocup_Basketball "2012–13 Eurocup Basketball"), the final was decided by a single game format, after double-legged semifinals and quarterfinals. For the [2013–14 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013%E2%80%9314_Eurocup_Basketball "2013–14 Eurocup Basketball"), the competition increased from 32 to 48 teams in the Regular Season phase. Another innovation that started in the 2013–14 season, was that the clubs were divided into two regional conferences, the Eastern Conference and the Western Conference, for the Regular Season phase.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_note-4) The size of the groups grew to six teams, where the first three qualified teams joined the Last 32 stage. In addition, the eight [EuroLeague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroLeague "EuroLeague") clubs that did not qualify for the EuroLeague Top 16 phase, joined the remaining 24 EuroCup teams and the Finals were decided by a double-legged series.

For the [2014–15 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Eurocup_Basketball "2014–15 Eurocup Basketball"), the competition contained 36 teams at the group stage. There were 6 groups, each containing 6 teams. The 36 teams consisted of the 7 teams that were eliminated in the [2014–15 Euroleague season qualification rounds](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Euroleague#Qualifying_rounds "2014–15 Euroleague"), and 29 teams that qualified directly to the 2014–15 EuroCup, either through 2013–14 season results, or through [wild cards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wild_card_(sports) "Wild card (sports)"). The top four teams from each of the Regular Season groups with the eight [EuroLeague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroLeague "EuroLeague") clubs that did not qualify for the EuroLeague Top 16 phase qualified to join the Last 32 stage. For the [2015–16 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Eurocup_Basketball "2015–16 Eurocup Basketball"), the competition contained 36 clubs automatically entered into the Regular Season and the eight [EuroLeague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroLeague "EuroLeague") clubs that did not qualify for the EuroLeague Top 16 phase qualified to join the Last 32 stage.

### European professional basketball club rankings

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: European professional basketball club rankings")]

Main article: [European professional basketball club rankings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/European_professional_basketball_club_rankings "European professional basketball club rankings")

### Arena standards

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Arena standards")]

Effective as of the [2012–13 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Eurocup_Basketball "2012–13 Eurocup Basketball"), all EuroCup clubs must host their home games in arenas that have a regular [seating capacity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seating_capacity "Seating capacity") of at least 2,500 (all seated), and an additional minimum capacity of 200 [VIP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/VIP "VIP") seats available.[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_note-5) By comparison, [EuroLeague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroLeague "EuroLeague") licensed clubs host their home games in arenas that seat at least 10,000 people, while EuroLeague associated clubs must have arenas that seat 5,000.

## Awards

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Awards")]

Main article: [EuroCup Basketball Awards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball_Awards "EuroCup Basketball Awards")

After a given EuroCup season, before the finals, annual EuroCup awards are handed out to players and coaches. These awards include:[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_note-6)

*   [Most Valuable Player](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball_MVP "EuroCup Basketball MVP")
*   [Finals MVP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball_Finals_MVP "EuroCup Basketball Finals MVP")
*   [Rising Star](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball_Rising_Star "EuroCup Basketball Rising Star")
*   [Coach of the Year](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball_Coach_of_the_Year "EuroCup Basketball Coach of the Year")
*   [All-EuroCup Team](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball_All-EuroCup_Team "EuroCup Basketball All-EuroCup Team")

## Performance by club

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Performance by club")]

[![Image 100](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e4/EuroCup_Basketball_regular_season.svg/500px-EuroCup_Basketball_regular_season.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:EuroCup_Basketball_regular_season.svg)

Map of countries, teams from which have reached the regular season of the EuroCup Basketball. 

Country that has been represented in the regular season

Not represented

Main article: [EuroCup Basketball records and statistics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball_records_and_statistics "EuroCup Basketball records and statistics")

A total number of 179 clubs from 30 countries have participated in the competition.

| Club | Winners | Runners-up | Years won | Years runner-up |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [![Image 101: Spain](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/9/9a/Flag_of_Spain.svg/40px-Flag_of_Spain.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spain "Spain")[Valencia Basket](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valencia_Basket "Valencia Basket") | 4 | 2 | [2002–03](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2002%E2%80%9303_ULEB_Cup "2002–03 ULEB Cup"), [2009–10](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2009%E2%80%9310_Eurocup_Basketball "2009–10 Eurocup Basketball"), [2013–14](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013%E2%80%9314_Eurocup_Basketball "2013–14 Eurocup Basketball"), [2018–19](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_EuroCup_Basketball "2018–19 EuroCup Basketball") | [2011–12](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_Eurocup_Basketball "2011–12 Eurocup Basketball"), [2016–17](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_EuroCup_Basketball "2016–17 EuroCup Basketball") |
| [![Image 102: Lithuania](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/11/Flag_of_Lithuania.svg/40px-Flag_of_Lithuania.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania")[Rytas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BC_Rytas "BC Rytas") | 2 | 1 | [2004–05](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2004%E2%80%9305_ULEB_Cup "2004–05 ULEB Cup"), [2008–09](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Eurocup_Basketball "2008–09 Eurocup Basketball") | [2006–07](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2006%E2%80%9307_ULEB_Cup "2006–07 ULEB Cup") |

| [![Image 119: Slovenia](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f0/Flag_of_Slovenia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Slovenia.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia")[Krka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KK_Krka "KK Krka") | 0 | 1 | – | [2002–03](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2002%E2%80%9303_ULEB_Cup "2002–03 ULEB Cup") |
| [![Image 120: Greece](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5c/Flag_of_Greece.svg/40px-Flag_of_Greece.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greece "Greece")[Makedonikos](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makedonikos_B.C. "Makedonikos B.C.") | 0 | 1 | – | [2004–05](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2004%E2%80%9305_ULEB_Cup "2004–05 ULEB Cup") |
| [![Image 121: Greece](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5c/Flag_of_Greece.svg/40px-Flag_of_Greece.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greece "Greece")[Aris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aris_B.C. "Aris B.C.") | 0 | 1 | – | [2005–06](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2005%E2%80%9306_ULEB_Cup "2005–06 ULEB Cup") |
| [![Image 122: Spain](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/9/9a/Flag_of_Spain.svg/40px-Flag_of_Spain.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spain "Spain")[Girona](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CB_Girona "CB Girona") | 0 | 1 | – | [2007–08](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_ULEB_Cup "2007–08 ULEB Cup") |
| [![Image 123: Spain](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/9/9a/Flag_of_Spain.svg/40px-Flag_of_Spain.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spain "Spain")[Real Betis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Real_Betis_Baloncesto "Real Betis Baloncesto") | 0 | 1 | – | [2010–11](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2010%E2%80%9311_Eurocup_Basketball "2010–11 Eurocup Basketball") |
| [![Image 124: Spain](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/9/9a/Flag_of_Spain.svg/40px-Flag_of_Spain.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spain "Spain")[Bilbao](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bilbao_Basket "Bilbao Basket") | 0 | 1 | – | [2012–13](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Eurocup_Basketball "2012–13 Eurocup Basketball") |
| [![Image 125: France](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/c3/Flag_of_France.svg/40px-Flag_of_France.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/France "France")[Strasbourg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SIG_Strasbourg "SIG Strasbourg") | 0 | 1 | – | [2015–16](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Eurocup_Basketball "2015–16 Eurocup Basketball") |
| [![Image 126: Turkey](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b4/Flag_of_Turkey.svg/40px-Flag_of_Turkey.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkey "Turkey")[Bursapor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bursaspor_Basketbol "Bursaspor Basketbol") | 0 | 1 | – | [2021–22](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_EuroCup_Basketball "2021–22 EuroCup Basketball") |
| [![Image 127: Turkey](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b4/Flag_of_Turkey.svg/40px-Flag_of_Turkey.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkey "Turkey")[Türk Telekom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/T%C3%BCrk_Telekom_B.K. "Türk Telekom B.K.") | 0 | 1 | – | [2022–23](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_EuroCup_Basketball "2021–22 EuroCup Basketball") |
| [![Image 128: France](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/c3/Flag_of_France.svg/40px-Flag_of_France.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/France "France")[Bourg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JL_Bourg_Basket "JL Bourg Basket") | 0 | 1 | – | [2023–24](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_EuroCup_Basketball "2023–24 EuroCup Basketball") |

## Performance by country

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Performance by country")]

Main article: [EuroCup Basketball records and statistics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball_records_and_statistics "EuroCup Basketball records and statistics")

| Rank | Nation | Champion | Finalist |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1. | [![Image 129: Spain](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/9/9a/Flag_of_Spain.svg/40px-Flag_of_Spain.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spain "Spain")**[Spain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spain "Spain")** | **8** **[Valencia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valencia_Basket "Valencia Basket")** (4), **[Real Madrid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Real_Madrid_Baloncesto "Real Madrid Baloncesto")** (1), **[Joventut](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joventut_Badalona "Joventut Badalona")** (1), **[Málaga](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baloncesto_M%C3%A1laga "Baloncesto Málaga")** (1), **[Gran Canaria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CB_Gran_Canaria "CB Gran Canaria")** (1) | **7** **[Valencia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valencia_Basket "Valencia Basket")** (2), **[Real Madrid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Real_Madrid_Baloncesto "Real Madrid Baloncesto")** (1), **[Girona](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CB_Sant_Josep "CB Sant Josep")** (1), **[Real Betis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Real_Betis_Baloncesto "Real Betis Baloncesto")** (1), **[Bilbao](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bilbao_Basket "Bilbao Basket")** (1), **[Gran Canaria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CB_Gran_Canaria "CB Gran Canaria")** (1) |
| 2. | [![Image 130: Russia](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/f/f3/Flag_of_Russia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Russia.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russia "Russia")**[Russia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russia "Russia")** | **5** **[Khimki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BC_Khimki "BC Khimki")** (2), **[Dynamo Moscow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MBC_Dynamo_Moscow "MBC Dynamo Moscow")** (1), **[UNICS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BC_UNICS "BC UNICS")** (1), **[Lokomotiv Kuban](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PBC_Lokomotiv_Kuban "PBC Lokomotiv Kuban")** (1) | **4** **[UNICS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BC_UNICS "BC UNICS")** (2), **[Khimki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BC_Khimki "BC Khimki")** (1), **[Lokomotiv Kuban](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PBC_Lokomotiv_Kuban "PBC Lokomotiv Kuban")** (1) |
| 3. | [![Image 131: Turkey](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b4/Flag_of_Turkey.svg/40px-Flag_of_Turkey.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkey "Turkey")**[Turkey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkey "Turkey")** | **2** **[Galatasaray](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Galatasaray_S.K._(men%27s_basketball) "Galatasaray S.K. (men's basketball)")** (1), **[Darüşşafaka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dar%C3%BC%C5%9F%C5%9Fafaka_S.K. "Darüşşafaka S.K.")** (1) | **2** **[Bursaspor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bursaspor_Basketbol "Bursaspor Basketbol")** (1), **[Türk Telekom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/T%C3%BCrk_Telekom_B.K. "Türk Telekom B.K.")** (1) |
| - | [![Image 132: France](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/c3/Flag_of_France.svg/40px-Flag_of_France.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/France "France")**[France](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/France "France")** | **2** **[Monaco](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AS_Monaco_Basket "AS Monaco Basket")** (1), **[Paris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paris_Basketball "Paris Basketball")** (1) | **2** **[Strasbourg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SIG_Strasbourg "SIG Strasbourg")** (1), **[Bourg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JL_Bourg_Basket "JL Bourg Basket")** (1) |

## Statistical leaders and individual high performances

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Statistical leaders and individual high performances")]

Main article: [EuroCup Basketball individual statistics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball_individual_statistics "EuroCup Basketball individual statistics")

### All-time leaders

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: All-time leaders")]

|  | **Average** | **Totals** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Points** | [![Image 139: Serbia](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/ff/Flag_of_Serbia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Serbia.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serbia "Serbia")[Igor Rakočević](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Igor_Rako%C4%8Devi%C4%87 "Igor Rakočević") | 19.05 | [![Image 140: Montenegro](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Flag_of_Montenegro.svg/40px-Flag_of_Montenegro.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro")[Bojan Dubljević](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bojan_Dubljevi%C4%87 "Bojan Dubljević") | 1,217 |
| **Rebounds** | [![Image 141: Montenegro](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Flag_of_Montenegro.svg/40px-Flag_of_Montenegro.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro")[Vladimir Golubović](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vladimir_Golubovi%C4%87 "Vladimir Golubović") | 8.39 | [![Image 142: Belarus](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/85/Flag_of_Belarus.svg/40px-Flag_of_Belarus.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Belarus "Belarus")[Vladimir Veremeenko](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vladimir_Veremeenko "Vladimir Veremeenko")[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_note-7) | 609 |
| **Assists** | [![Image 143: Montenegro](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Flag_of_Montenegro.svg/40px-Flag_of_Montenegro.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro")[Omar Cook](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Omar_Cook "Omar Cook") | 6.44 | [![Image 144: Serbia](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/ff/Flag_of_Serbia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Serbia.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serbia "Serbia")[Stefan Marković](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stefan_Markovi%C4%87_(basketball) "Stefan Marković (basketball)") | 491 |
| **Steals** | [![Image 145: United States](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/Flag_of_the_United_States.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States "United States")[Jerry McCullough](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jerry_McCullough "Jerry McCullough") | 2.82 | [![Image 146: United States](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/Flag_of_the_United_States.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States "United States")[Mire Chatman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mire_Chatman "Mire Chatman") | 167 |
| **Blocks** | [![Image 147: United States](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/Flag_of_the_United_States.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States "United States")[Andre Riddick](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andre_Riddick "Andre Riddick") | 1.77 | [![Image 148: United States](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/Flag_of_the_United_States.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States "United States")[Andre Riddick](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andre_Riddick "Andre Riddick") | 147 |
| **Index Ratings** | [![Image 149: Turkey](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b4/Flag_of_Turkey.svg/40px-Flag_of_Turkey.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkey "Turkey")[Michael Wright](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michael_Wright_(basketball) "Michael Wright (basketball)") | 22.14 | [![Image 150: United States](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/Flag_of_the_United_States.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States "United States")[Mire Chatman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mire_Chatman "Mire Chatman") | 1,472 |

### Highest attendance records

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Highest attendance records")]

*   **24,232** attendance for [Red Star Belgrade](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KK_Red_Star_Belgrade "KK Red Star Belgrade") in a 79–70 win over [Budivelnyk Kyiv](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BC_Budivelnyk_Kyiv "BC Budivelnyk Kyiv"), at [Kombank Arena](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kombank_Arena "Kombank Arena"), Belgrade, on 26 March 2014.[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_note-8)
*   **22,736** attendance for [Red Star Belgrade](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KK_Red_Star_Belgrade "KK Red Star Belgrade") in a 63–52 win over [UNICS Kazan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BC_UNICS "BC UNICS"), at [Kombank Arena](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kombank_Arena "Kombank Arena"), [Belgrade](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Belgrade "Belgrade"), on 2 April 2014.

## Sponsors

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Sponsors")]

### Title sponsor

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Title sponsor")]

*   [BKT](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Balkrishna_Industries "Balkrishna Industries")

### Premium partners

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Premium partners")]

*   [Turkish Airlines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkish_Airlines "Turkish Airlines")
*   Tempobet (only in Germany)
*   [Fonbet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fonbet "Fonbet") (only in Russia)
*   Nesine (only in Turkey)
*   [betfair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betfair "Betfair") (only in Spain)
*   [sportingbet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sportingbet "Sportingbet") (only in Greece)
*   [Adidas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adidas "Adidas")

### Global partners

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Global partners")]

*   [Spalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spalding_(sports_equipment) "Spalding (sports equipment)")
*   Odeabank (only in Turkey)
*   [Head & Shoulders](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Head_%26_Shoulders "Head & Shoulders") (only in Turkey)
*   SEK (only in Turkey)
*   [Oscar Mayer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oscar_Mayer "Oscar Mayer") (only in Spain)
*   [Endesa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endesa "Endesa") (only in Spain)

[Euroleague Basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball"). Archived from [the original](http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/global) on 7 November 2017. Retrieved 4 November 2017.
10.   **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_ref-10)**["Germany – Marketing Partners"](https://web.archive.org/web/20171107031248/http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/germany). [Euroleague Basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball"). Archived from [the original](http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/germany) on 7 November 2017. Retrieved 4 November 2017.
11.   **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_ref-11)**["Russia – Marketing Partners"](https://web.archive.org/web/20171107031819/http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/russia). [Euroleague Basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball"). Archived from [the original](http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/russia) on 7 November 2017. Retrieved 4 November 2017.
12.   **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_ref-12)**["Turkey – Marketing Partners"](https://web.archive.org/web/20171107055240/http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/turkey). [Euroleague Basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball"). Archived from [the original](http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/turkey) on 7 November 2017. Retrieved 4 November 2017.
13.   **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_ref-13)**["Spain – Marketing Partners"](https://web.archive.org/web/20171107031836/http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/spain). [Euroleague Basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball"). Archived from [the original](http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/spain) on 7 November 2017. Retrieved 4 November 2017.
14.   **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Basketball#cite_ref-14)**["Greece – Marketing Partners"](https://web.archive.org/web/20171107030457/http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/greece). [Euroleague Basketball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball"). Archived from [the original](http://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/marketing-partners/greece) on 7 November 2017. Retrieved 4 November 2017.

## See also

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: See also")]

### Men's competitions

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Men's competitions")]

*   [EuroLeague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroLeague "EuroLeague")
*   [Basketball Champions League](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basketball_Champions_League "Basketball Champions League")
*   [FIBA Europe Cup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA_Europe_Cup "FIBA Europe Cup")

### Women's competitions

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=EuroCup_Basketball&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Women's competitions")]

*   [EuroLeague Women](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroLeague_Women "EuroLeague Women")
*   [EuroCup Women](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EuroCup_Women "EuroCup Women")
*   [FIBA Europe SuperCup Women](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA_Europe_SuperCup_Women "FIBA Europe SuperCup Women")

"Japan Basketball League") * Taiwan * [Chinese Basketball Alliance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chinese_Basketball_Alliance "Chinese Basketball Alliance") * [T1 League](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/T1_League "T1 League") | | Europe | * _[SuproLeague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2000%E2%80%9301\_FIBA\_SuproLeague "2000–01 FIBA SuproLeague")_ * _[Saporta Cup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA\_Saporta\_Cup "FIBA Saporta Cup")_ * _[Korać Cup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA\_Kora%C4%87\_Cup "FIBA Korać Cup")_ * _[EuroChallenge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA\_EuroChallenge "FIBA EuroChallenge")_ * _[EuroCup Challenge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIBA\_EuroCup\_Challenge "FIBA EuroCup Challenge")_ * [Alpe Adria Cup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alpe_Adria_Cup "Alpe Adria Cup") * [Baltic League](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baltic_Basketball_League "Baltic Basketball League") * [Central European League](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_European_Basketball_League "Central European Basketball League") * [Latin Cup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Latin_Cup_(basketball) "Latin Cup (basketball)") * [North European League](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/North_European_Basketball_League "North European Basketball League") * [Czechoslovakia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Czechoslovak_Basketball_League "Czechoslovak Basketball League") * [England](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Basketball_League_(England) "National Montenegro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/YUBA_League "YUBA League") * [2nd](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/YUBA_B_League "YUBA B League") *
